senses.definition: the ability in a person that makes them think, feel, or imagine

examples.x: She has a brilliant mind for solving puzzles.
Meditation helps in calming the mind.
His sharp mind allowed him to excel in academic pursuits.
The human mind is capable of incredible creativity.
Reading stimulates the mind and broadens one's perspective.

senses.definition: happening or related to someone's mind, involving thoughts, feelings, and cognitive processes

examples.x: She experienced a mental breakthrough during the meditation session.
The puzzle requires significant mental effort to solve.
He keeps a mental list of all the tasks he needs to complete.

senses.definition: an ability that a person naturally has in doing something well

examples.x: His talent for playing the piano was evident from a young age.
She discovered her talent for painting when she was just a child.
The singer's remarkable talent for hitting high notes amazed the audience.
Sarah's talent for problem-solving made her an invaluable member of the team.
The gymnast's talent for flexibility and strength earned her many medals.

senses.definition: an ability to do something well, especially after training

examples.x: After years of practice, her skill in playing the guitar became exceptional.
His skill in coding allowed him to develop innovative software applications.
The chef's skill in culinary arts resulted in mouthwatering dishes.
She honed her skill in photography through years of dedicated practice.
The athlete's skill in dribbling and shooting made him a star player on the basketball team.

senses.definition: an understanding of or information about a subject after studying and experiencing it

examples.x: His knowledge of history allowed him to provide insightful explanations during the discussion.
Her knowledge of physics helped her solve complex problems in her research.
Jane gained knowledge about ancient history by reading books and visiting museums.
Experience working in the field provided Sarah with valuable knowledge about environmental conservation.
Access to the internet allows us to acquire knowledge on a wide range of topics with just a few clicks.

senses.definition: an attempt to give an answer without having enough facts

examples.x: Sarah made a guess about the number of jellybeans in the jar at the fair.
Without any evidence, Tom's guess about the outcome of the game was purely speculative.
Emily took a wild guess at the answer to the difficult math problem.
Sam's guess about the weather tomorrow was based on the clouds he saw outside.

senses.definition: to consider something as true without being sure

examples.x: I guess it might rain later, so bring an umbrella.
I'm not sure, but I'll guess it's around 3 o'clock.
I guess he'll be here in about 10 minutes.

senses.definition: to accept something to be true even without proof

examples.x: I believed her excuses for missing the meeting.
For a long time, I believed his exaggerated tales of adventure.
I find it hard to believe that she won the lottery twice in a row.
They were misled into believing the product was a miracle cure.
You shouldn't believe everything you see on social media.

senses.definition: something that we think is true or real

examples.x: Many people hold the belief that honesty is the best policy.
The explorer's discovery challenged the prevailing beliefs about the origin of the river.
In some cultures, there are deeply held beliefs about the significance of certain symbols or rituals.
His belief in karma led him to always act with kindness towards others.
He expressed his belief in the importance of education for societal progress.

senses.definition: the ability of mind to keep and remember past events, people, experiences, etc.

examples.x: She relied on her memory to recall the lyrics to her favorite songs during karaoke night.
Studying and sleep are important for improving memory.
He has a photographic memory and can recall information with ease.
She has a great memory for faces.
Alzheimer's disease can affect memory and cognitive functions.

senses.definition: to bring a type of information from the past to our mind again

examples.x: Can you remember the name of the book we were talking about?
I remember the day we first met; it was a sunny afternoon.
I remember the smell of freshly baked cookies in my grandmother's kitchen.
I'll always remember our graduation day.
We remember our childhood memories fondly.

senses.definition: to not be able to remember something or someone from the past

examples.x: It's easy to forget passwords, so it's essential to use a secure system.
Try not to forget the main points of your presentation.
She often forgets details about events from her early years.
He tried to remember his childhood friend, but he had forgotten her name.
He will never forget the kindness you showed him.

senses.definition: a suggestion or thought about something that we could do

examples.x: Sarah proposed a brilliant idea for the upcoming fundraiser
Do you have any ideas on how to improve our team's productivity?
Let's brainstorm and come up with creative ideas for the marketing campaign.
I appreciate your input; your idea about streamlining the process is worth considering.
The manager welcomed any ideas from the employees to enhance workplace morale.

senses.definition: liked or preferred the most among the rest that are from the same category

examples.x: His favorite hobby is playing guitar in his free time.
The romantic comedy genre is her favorite when it comes to movies.
Pizza is always a crowd favorite at parties.

senses.definition: the act of putting an idea or plan forward for someone to think about

examples.x: His suggestion to streamline the company's workflow was well-received by the team.
We welcome any suggestions for improving our customer service.
The professor encouraged students to share their suggestions for topics to cover in the course.
After listening to everyone's suggestions, they finally agreed on a restaurant for dinner.
I appreciate your suggestion to try meditation as a stress-relief technique.

senses.definition: to mention an idea, proposition, plan, etc. for further consideration or possible action

examples.x: The committee suggested changes to improve the efficiency of the process.
The teacher suggested additional resources for better understanding the topic.
The professor suggested several topics for research papers in the upcoming semester.
She suggested a new approach to solving the problem.
The committee suggested changes to the draft proposal.

senses.definition: our purpose or desired result

examples.x: Her goal is to become a successful entrepreneur and start her own business.
Setting achievable goals is essential for personal growth and development.
The team's primary goal is to win the championship this season.
He worked tirelessly to achieve his lifelong goal of climbing Mount Everest.
Setting short-term goals can help break down larger tasks into manageable steps.

senses.definition: a chain of actions that will help us reach our goals

examples.x: The project manager presented a detailed plan outlining the phases of the construction.
We need to devise a strategic plan to expand our market presence.
The business proposal includes a comprehensive financial plan for the next fiscal year.
Emily shared her plan for organizing a successful charity event.
The team is working on a contingency plan to address potential challenges in the project.

senses.definition: to want something to happen or be true

examples.x: They hoped their team would win the championship.
She hoped he would forgive her for the mistake.
He hopes that his hard work will be recognized and rewarded.
As the election approaches, many citizens hope for positive changes in government.
The team is practicing diligently, hoping to win the championship.

senses.definition: a feeling of expectation and desire for a particular thing to happen or to be true

examples.x: Despite the challenges, she held onto hope for a better future.
His hope for peace in the region kept him working tirelessly for diplomatic solutions.
The child's eyes sparkled with hope as she made a wish upon a shooting star.
We should never lose hope, even in the face of adversity.
The discovery of a potential treatment gave hope to patients suffering from the disease.

senses.definition: to make or have an image of something in our mind

examples.x: Close your eyes and imagine a beautiful sunset over the ocean.
Can you imagine a world without technology?
She likes to imagine herself traveling to exotic destinations while reading a book.
As a child, he used to imagine being a superhero and saving the day.

senses.definition: to experience something in our mind while we are asleep

examples.x: Last night, I dreamt of flying over a beautiful landscape.
Every night, she dreams of exploring distant galaxies and meeting extraterrestrial beings.
Dreaming allows the mind to explore scenarios that may not be possible in reality.
She dreamt of being able to breathe underwater.

senses.definition: to take pleasure or find happiness in something or someone

examples.x: She enjoys listening to classical music while working.
We enjoyed a delicious meal at the new restaurant in town.
He often enjoys hiking in the mountains during the weekends.
We enjoyed the movie so much that we watched it twice.
Despite the rain, they enjoyed the outdoor concert.

senses.definition: feeling unhappy and afraid because of something that has happened or might happen

examples.x: She was worried about her upcoming exams, feeling anxious about whether she had studied enough.
He was worried about his daughter's safety, feeling anxious about her late return home.
She was worried about her financial situation, feeling uneasy about her mounting debts.
They were worried about their health, feeling anxious about the results of their medical tests.
He was worried about his job security, feeling uneasy about the company's recent layoffs.

senses.definition: tending to easily get worried or frightened about things

examples.x: The nervous cat hid under the couch when visitors arrived.
The nervous child clung to their parent's leg in the unfamiliar environment.
Despite being a nervous puppy, he gradually became more confident with training.
Despite her friend's reassurances, the nervous traveler couldn't shake off her anxiety about flying.
The anxious, nervous student fidgeted in his seat during the exam.

senses.definition: not showing worry, anger, or other strong emotions

examples.x: Despite the chaos around her, she remained calm and composed.
He took a deep breath to stay calm during the stressful situation.
The calm expression on her face masked any inner turmoil she might have felt.
In the face of adversity, she approached the challenge with a calm mindset.
Even when criticized, he responded in a calm and collected manner.

senses.definition: getting a bad and anxious feeling from a person or thing because we think something bad or dangerous will happen

examples.x: He was afraid of heights, feeling anxious and uneasy when standing on tall buildings or cliffs.
She was afraid of walking alone in the dark, feeling anxious about potential dangers lurking in the shadows.
They were afraid of the dark, feeling anxious and uncomfortable when the lights were off.
She was afraid of spiders, experiencing a sense of fear and panic whenever she saw one.
He was afraid of flying, experiencing a sense of fear and unease when boarding an airplane.

senses.definition: the skill and knowledge we gain from doing, feeling, or seeing things

examples.x: Her years of experience as a chef have made her an expert in the kitchen.
Traveling to different countries broadens one's cultural experiences.
He shared his experiences with overcoming obstacles during his inspirational speech.
The team's collective experience in project management ensured the successful completion of the task.
Life experience teaches us valuable lessons that we carry with us throughout our lives.

senses.definition: a situation or subject that needs to be dealt with or considered

examples.x: Climate change is a pressing matter that requires immediate attention from global leaders.
The disagreement between the two parties became a legal matter that required resolution in court.
Choosing a college major is a significant matter that requires careful consideration.
His health had become a matter of concern for his family.
The matter of budget allocation was discussed during the meeting.

senses.definition: to think carefully about different things and choose one of them

examples.x: He had to decide whether to accept the job offer.
After much debate, they decided to go on a road trip.
I couldn't decide between pizza or pasta, so I ordered both.

senses.definition: the act of reaching a choice or judgement after careful consideration

examples.x: The moment of decision had come, and she knew she had to choose between two paths.
The power of decision remained with the shareholders, who ultimately determined the company's policies.
With multiple options on the table, the moment of decision approached for the board of trustees.
In the company, the power of decision rested solely with the CEO, whose word was final.

senses.definition: the ability to do or achieve something

examples.x: The president holds the power to veto legislation passed by Congress.
Renewable energy sources have the power to reduce our dependence on fossil fuels.
The internet has transformed the way we communicate, giving individuals the power to connect globally.
With determination and hard work, individuals have the power to overcome adversity.
Technology has empowered individuals with the power to access vast amounts of information instantly.

senses.definition: easy to understand

examples.x: His instructions were clear, allowing everyone to follow them without confusion.
The presentation slides were clear, with concise bullet points and relevant visuals.
The map was clear, showing all the important landmarks and routes.
The explanation provided by the doctor was clear, alleviating the patient's concerns.
The rules of the game were clear, making it easy for newcomers to join.

senses.definition: an act of deciding to choose between two things or more

examples.x: The menu offered a wide choice of dishes.
He was faced with a difficult choice between two careers.
Their choice of music was perfect for the party.
This coat is a great choice for cold weather.
Parents always want the best choices for their children.

senses.definition: to recognize or become aware of a particular scent

examples.x: I always smell the aroma of fresh coffee when I enter the cafe.
She often smells the scent of roses in her garden.
Yesterday, I smelled a delicious fragrance coming from the bakery.
Why are you always smelling the air after it rains?
Right now, I am smelling the flowers in the botanical garden.

senses.definition: to bring a memory back to a person's mind

examples.x: A photograph can often remind individuals of cherished moments.
The scent of freshly baked cookies can remind people of childhood.
A familiar song on the radio can remind someone of a special occasion.
The scenic view from the hill reminded him of a similar landscape from his travels.
The old photograph reminded her of the happy moments spent with friends.

senses.definition: the likelihood of experiencing harm, damage, or injury

examples.x: Drinking and driving poses a danger.
Firefighters face danger when entering burning buildings.
Ignoring lab safety rules is risky and can lead to danger.
The warning signs along the beach alerted swimmers to the danger of strong currents.
